rationale: Title Y is more JACC-ready because it is concise, finding-driven, and immediately tells readers the main clinical signal: similar success with increased paravalvular leak. Title X is precise and appropriately formal, but it reads like a registry descriptor and does not communicate the key result.
loser_weakness: Title X is too long and outcome-generic. It names the population and registry well, but it misses the main finding and has lower editorial impact.
